title: Hattie and the Lego Aquarium Adventure
summary: Hattie the mouse and her friends Clownfish and Splownfish decide to build a grand Lego aquarium in the living room while waiting for their hot stew to cool down. The aquarium becomes a bustling home for an anglerfish and ten other uniquely named fishes. After Hattie joins them for a swim, the whole group boards a musical trolley for an excursion to the mousey playground. Following a day of play, everyone returns home to put on their pajamas and drift off to sleep to a gentle lullaby.

highlightQuote.text: Hey, it's not snack time everybody. It's lunch time. Oh, but now it's playground time on the trolley! Ding-ding! Time for a ride!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, alright, now it's time to turn on the stars and turn out the light so that we can do...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, the story is always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, remember what I said: keep telling the story and then you'll find out. Dad, remember what I said?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, wait, what did you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I said, keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then that's how you find out what happens, that's true, by telling the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse made friends with two creatures whose names were Clownfish and Splownfish.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Dad, keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll keep telling the story. And so, Hattie and Clownfish and Splownfish decided to do a project together. What were they going to do together?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, Mom said it... their stew was very, very hot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was very hot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Their stew.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Their stew. Oh. Clownfish and Splownfish said to Hattie, "We're having stew for dinner, but it's too hot. What could we do about that?" And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But we want to do a project, so we're coming in the kitchen. Or otherwise, we won't have time to do a project.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Um, what kind of project should we do? said Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, don't come in the kitchen. You have to do a project in the living room.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Clownfish and Splownfish said, "Alright, we'll go in the living room and do our project here. We could build something, Hattie. What should we build?" And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And then Hattie said, "Oh, you could build an aquarium, couldn't you?" And Clownfish and Splownfish said, "Sure, right away. We'll build an aquarium right here in the living room. We could build it out of Legos." And Hattie said, "Alright, well, I'll just keep working on this stew. Let me know when you're done." Um, and what did Clownfish and Splownfish build?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A big, big pot... no, a big, big aquarium. Abarium? Aquarium. Abarium? No, aquarium.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did it have in it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A big fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what kind of fish was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was an anglerfish. Very nice anglerfish. Did bad things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a nice anglerfish? Oh. It did bad things. What kind of things did it do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Knocked over all kind of things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it knocked over things. Oh, that's bad. And what else did the anglerfish do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, now I can jump off of my stool.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, because the stars and stories lantern is on...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, Dad, can you make it take it off for a minute and then jump?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We don't do jumps during stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, just one... for one minute.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! And... there. Dad, now what's... now you have to sit in my lap in this chair because it has a very big pillow in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It does have a big pillow. Now, I have to sit in your lap?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. No, no, I have to sit in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see what you mean. That's true. Bum-ba-bum, ba-bum.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now you can tell the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, so Clownfish and Splownfish were in the living room building their aquarium and it had a big, very nice anglerfish and what else did it have in the aquarium that they built?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some four bad, bad, bad, bad, bad, bad fishes. Bad fishes. And there were 10 fishes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what were their names?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bumpfish, and Trips, and Bumpfish, and this Bumpfish, and this Ups-umps fish, and this Sour-ish fish, and this Bat-down-ey fish. And then there were five.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And this was Bat-down-ey-umps fish, this was Rum-some fish, this was Ro-lee-pops fish, this was Ba-tally fish, this was Joe-fish, and this was Up-peit-y fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. That's amazing. That's an aquarium.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie! Um, there are all kind of fishes live there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, "Wow, I'm just finishing up in the kitchen. Let me come check out your aquarium." And so she came in the living room and she saw the aquarium and then she said, "Oh, wow, can I try it? Can I go in and swim with these fish?" And what did Clownfish and Splownfish say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can swim with us.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, thanks!" And Hattie put on her bathing suit and she swam, splash, right into the aquarium. And then all the fishes said, "Hey, we're hungry. Do you have any fish dinner?" And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "You don't have any fish dinner? What are we going to eat?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, it's not snack time everybody. It's lunch time. Oh, but now it's playground time on the trolley! Ding-ding! Time for a ride!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And all the fish got onto the trolley to go to the fish playground.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie and all the other fishes. Everyone aboard!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All aboard the trolley! Ding-ding!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And their mother, and their dada, and the baby, and Gina, and... their puppet, and their Nana, and their Emily, and their Julian. Choo-choo everyone! All aboard says the mousey playground.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, and everyone got to go to the playground all together on the trolley slash train that went ding-ding choo-choo all aboard.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Choo-choo-choo, ding-ding-ding, we're at the playground. Everyone out!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, and everyone got out at the playground and what did they do on the playground?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They played and played and played until it was time to go home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then they all went back home and they all felt very, very sleepy. And so they all put on their pajamas...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Choo-choo, we're back home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We're back home. And they put on their pajamas and they turned out the lights and they sang a little lullaby. How did the lullaby go?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then they got in their cribs and they went to sleep. The end.

characters.name: Hattie the mouse
characters.description: A sociable companion to Mickey and Minnie, this mouse finds joy in gathering friends over hearty pots of homemade stew or setting off on spirited explorations, such as her recent journey through the shimmering wonders of a deep-sea aquarium.

characters.name: Clownfish
characters.description: One of Hattie's friends who helps build a Lego aquarium.

characters.name: Splownfish
characters.description: One of Hattie's friends who helps build a Lego aquarium.

characters.name: Anglerfish
characters.description: A cantankerous lurker of the sunless depths, this creature guards his silence with a perpetual scowl, craving only the stillness of a long slumber. Yet, beneath his prickly veneer lies a well-meaning mischief-maker who frequently shatters the very quiet he seeks, playfully toppling artifacts with a flick of his fins before retreating back into his grumpy solitude.

characters.name: Bumpfish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Trips
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Ups-umps fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Sour-ish fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Bat-down-ey fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Bat-down-ey-umps fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Rum-some fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Ro-lee-pops fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Ba-tally fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Joe-fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Up-peit-y fish
characters.description: One of the ten fishes in the aquarium.

characters.name: Gina
characters.description: A small, cerulean baby angler fish of the deep-sea family, now seen swaying gently as a passenger aboard the clattering playground trolley.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Emily
characters.description: A steady-handed passenger on the playground trolley, this loyal companion bears the faint, tacky traces of adhesive on their fingertips—a testament to the patience required to painstakingly mend a shattered sculpture and restore beauty from its broken shards.

characters.name: Julian
characters.description: A loyal Martian local and trusted confidant to the crew, this indigo-colored elephant friend is adorned with shimmering stripes and a dusting of glitter. A steadfast source of support for Melly, they are frequently found navigating the crimson dunes of the red planet as a serene passenger aboard the whimsical playground trolley.

characters.name: Baby
characters.description: A passenger on the playground trolley.

places.name: Kitchen
places.description: Within this shared sanctuary, Finncat keeps a watchful eye as Hattie tends to the hearth, the air growing thick with the savory, comforting steam of the hot stew she prepares.

places.name: Living room
places.description: A soft-lit alcove imbued with the memory of Jane and Silver’s first snuggle, its floor now a vibrant landscape of interlocking bricks as they construct their ambitious Lego aquarium project.

places.name: Lego Aquarium
places.description: A large aquarium built out of blocks that characters can swim in.

places.name: Mousey playground
places.description: The destination where all the fishes and friends go to play.

places.name: Trolley
places.description: A musical transport that takes everyone to the playground.
